message.content:
=== THE LAW ===
A source enters the corpus ONLY bound to real bytes via provenance.py (manifestation_id + content_hash).
Under a JOURNAL-ONLY contract it must be journal-attributable. A working paper / preprint / ACCEPTED
MANUSCRIPT is NOT the journal version -- peer review changes numbers (Acemoglu's robot effect: 0.37pp in
the NBER WP, 0.2pp in the published JPE). `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` must stay GREEN;
NEVER weaken a check to make it pass. WE DO NOT USE SCI-HUB/LIBGEN -- only legitimate green-OA.

=== TONIGHT'S LESSON, EARNED FOUR TIMES ===
THE BUILDER CANNOT VERIFY ITSELF. Modules self-tested green while fabrication shipped; provenance.py
passed 18/18 while the P0 ran live on disk; the canary went 16/16 while 6 attacks succeeded. So: the
agent that builds does NOT verify. A separate adversary attacks.


=== SOL'S DESIGN IS AT /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_FULLTEXT_LANE_V9.md — READ IT FIRST. IT IS THE BUILD CONTRACT. DO NOT REDESIGN IT. ===

Why this lane: our corpus is TEN journal-attributable works. cellcog (#1) has ~98 sources. The argument
planner (the biggest score lever) found ZERO genuine cross-source conflicts in 10 papers -- it is STARVED.
Sol's yield forecast for the ~2,490 no-known-OA candidates: 450-850 complete documents, 180-360 STRICT
journal-VoR-attributable, best estimate ~260 additional strict journal full texts. The three biggest lanes
(CORE 60-140, OpenAIRE 45-110, OAI-PMH 35-90) ARE ALL UNBUILT.

Discovery is DONE: ~5,061 candidates in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/ (frontier_2023_2025_FINAL,
oa_cell_discovery, canon_and_contrasts_leads, citation-graph). ~2,571 already carry an offline OA URL.
Existing tools: acquisition.py (the ONE network door), provenance.py, event_ledger.py, source_router.py +
config/source_routes.yaml, deep_fetch.py, weighting.py.


The P0 was just closed: Done. All five items closed, gate green, nothing committed.

## What changed (file:line)

**1. The accepted-manuscript P0 — killed at all three hops**
- `scripts/provenance.py:216` → `SPAN_PRESERVING = ('exact_copy_of',)`. Added `NON_SPAN_PRESERVING` so "which edges are inert" is a statement, not an absence from a tuple.
- `scripts/version_align.py:220` → mapping kept (the label is real evidence) but made **inert**: added `span_preserving()` which reads `provenance.SPAN_PRESERVING` rather than restating it, plus an import-time `assert` that fails loudly if `accepted_manuscript_of` ever returns to the tuple. The `ALIGNED_FULL` note no longer says "Edge X may be ASSERTED" for an accepted manus

YOUR TASK — SOL §7 (the persistent host scheduler) + §2 (targeted OAI-PMH, 35-90 yield).

A. PERSISTENT CROSS-PROCESS SCHEDULER. Replace _HOST_LAST and the single SPACING_S in acquisition.py:
   per-host TOKEN BUCKET + concurrency limit; resolver host and content host budgeted SEPARATELY (a
   redirect charges the DESTINATION host); limits enforced from source_routes.yaml; DYNAMIC LIMITS MAY
   ONLY REDUCE the configured rate; parse BOTH numeric and HTTP-date Retry-After; store `not_before` —
   "do not sleep and retry three seconds later when the server requests hours"; cache exact-ID resolver
   responses (ETag/Last-Modified); batch PMC identifiers; CIRCUIT-BREAK on repeated 401/403/429/5xx;
   parallelise ACROSS independent hosts, never within a host beyond its policy. Must be safe across
   MULTIPLE WORKER PROCESSES (we will run this detached and resumable).
   OUTCOME SEMANTICS, exactly: 429 -> THROTTLED (deferred) | 401 -> AUTH_FAILED | 403 -> ACCESS_DENIED
   (that URL) | timeout/5xx -> bounded retry then BACKEND_FAILED.
   ** NONE of these may EVER reduce to "no OA copy exists." ** (We got IP-throttled all night; and a 403
   is publisher access-control, not absence.)

B. TARGETED OAI-PMH. Do NOT attempt global per-work OAI harvesting. Obtain the OAI identifier + repository
   base URL through CORE / OpenAIRE / repository metadata / a local index, then GetRecord(identifier,
   metadataPrefix). Rich formats (JATS/METS/MODS/OAI-ORE) may expose document URLs; oai_dc commonly
   exposes only metadata or a LANDING PAGE. Default 1 in flight, 2s interval until the repository row says
   otherwise; obey Retry-After, robots, resumption tokens, deletion records. EACH REPOSITORY IS A DATA ROW
   (base URL, metadata prefixes, identifier mappings, file selectors, rate policy).
   Silent failures: oai_dc landing page treated as a PDF; a deleted record treated as "no evidence in the
   world"; the DOI belonging to a CITED REFERENCE; a repository COVER SHEET hiding a different article.

Canary green. Prove the scheduler holds under 2+ concurrent worker processes. Do NOT commit.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""ACQUISITION — THE ONLY DOOR TO THE NETWORK, AND IT CANNOT CONCLUDE.

    Sol (a)(1): "ACQUISITION EMITS OBSERVATIONS AND MANIFESTATIONS, NEVER STATUSES."

THE FAILURE THIS IS THE STRUCTURAL CURE FOR

  Tonight a forced HTTP 429 on Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) — whose free copy provably exists, NBER
  Working Paper 8337, in full, forever — was written to disk as `content_status = CITATION_ONLY`.
  CITATION_ONLY is the MINER'S EXCLUSION LABEL. A transient throttle PERMANENTLY DELETED the
  most-cited paper in the literature from our evidence base.

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SOURCE ROUTES — the DECLARATIVE, CAPABILITY-KEYED discovery table (Sol plan 4, item 2).
#
# THE STRATEGY THIS ENCODES
#   The old strategy — "choose the canonical (usually paywalled) work, then chase a free copy of it" —
#   had a 19% hit rate, because it started from the version we were LEAST likely to be able to read.
#   This table encodes the inverse: OA-FIRST DISCOVERY, then VERSION PURSUIT. We enumerate what is
#   openly readable FIRST (DOAJ / PMC / Europe PMC / OpenAlex / CORE / OpenAIRE), we resolve identity
#   and the full set of locations (Crossref / OpenAlex / Unpaywall — and we NEVER treat their version
#   label as final), and only then do we pursue the specific citable expression the plan's policy
#   requires (e.g. the journal Version of Record for a journal-only question).
#
# WHY THIS IS DATA AND NOT CODE
#   The router MUST NOT "know" that economists post to NBER or that clinicians register trials at
#   ClinicalTrials.gov. That knowledge is a hardcoded topic gate — the exact tell this project keeps
#   failing on. So it lives HERE, as ONE ROW PER ROUTE, keyed by the EVIDENCE CAPABILITY the route
#   supplies. Adding a domain that these adapters already serve (a new jurisdiction's courts, a new
#   preprint server) is a NEW ROW in this file and ZERO lines of Python. scripts/source_router.py is a
#   generic matcher + set-cover engine over this table; it contains no adapter name and no topic word.
#
# HOW A QUESTION REACHES A ROUTE  (all three stages are data-driven; see source_router.py)
#   1. compile_contract(question)                       -> a research contract (research_contract.py)
#   2. role_requirements  (this file)  x  contract      -> the REQUIRED evidence roles for this plan
#   3. SET COVER  required_roles  over  routes[*].evidence_roles -> the routes that FIRE
#   The admissibility of what a route returns is a SEPARATE axis (source_policy over document_types):
#   a preprint route may FIRE for discovery/version-pursuit yet yield nothing CITABLE under a
#   journal-only policy. Firing and citability are never the same decision.
#
# EVERY LIVE ATTEMPT GOES THROUGH acquisition.Acquirer, so it lands on the event ledger and the
# distinct outcomes stay distinct:
#   FETCHED | NOT_FOUND | ACCESS_DENIED | THROTTLED | TRANSIENT_ERROR | LANDING_PAGE | ABSTRACT_ONLY |
#   WRONG_WORK | CORRUPT_EXTRACTION.
# Only NOT_FOUND, across every APPLICABLE route that genuinely answered, supports "we did not locate an
# accessible copy." A 429 (THROTTLED) never does, and neither does a 403 (ACCESS_DENIED).
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

schema_version: 1
registry_version: sr-1
mailto_env: POLARIS_MAILTO          # the polite-pool contact the router threads into query dialects
fetch_date: "2026-07-14"

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# THE ROLE VOCABULARY — an EVIDENCE CAPABILITY, not a domain. A route SUPPLIES roles; a plan REQUIRES
# roles; set cover joins them. The four `discover.*/resolve.*/fetch.*` roles are the OA-first backbone
# every scholarly plan needs. The `evidence.*` roles are field-scoped and fire only when the plan's own
# vocabulary calls for them. The dotted `capability.field` form is deliberate: the capability is the
# noun, the field is what a new domain edits.
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
roles:
  # -- OA-first backbone (always required for a scholarly artifact) -----------------------------------
  discover.index:        "Enumerate candidate scholarly works by topic (a bibliographic index)."
  resolve.identity:      "Resolve a work's identity, dates and version RELATIONS from an identifier. NOT full text."
  resolve.locations:     "Enumerate the OA copy LOCATIONS/versions of a known work. The version label is a hint, never final."
  fetch.oa_fulltext:     "Retrieve open-access FULL TEXT (XML/PDF/HTML) that we can actually read and mine."
  # -- field-scoped evidence kinds (fire on the plan's own evidence vocabulary) -----------------------
  evidence.peer_reviewed:          "Peer-reviewed journal articles — the citable class under a journal-only / peer-reviewed policy."
  evidence.working_paper.economics: "Economics/policy working papers as SEPARATE works (peer review changes the numbers)."
  evidence.preprint.general:       "General/CS/physics/math preprints as separate works."
  evidence.preprint.biomedical:    "Biomedical/health preprints as separate works."
  evidence.working_paper.social_legal: "Social-science and legal scholarship / working papers."
  evidence.trial_registration:     "Prospective clinical-trial registrations and results."
  evidence.clinical_guideline:     "Clinical practice guidelines and evidence syntheses from guideline bodies."
  evidence.case_law:               "Primary case law — judicial opinions and their holdings."
  evidence.statute_regulation:     "Primary statutes, regulations and official legal instruments."
  evidence.regulatory_guidance:    "Regulator guidance, decisions and official filings."
  evidence.standard:               "Technical standards and normative specifications."
  evidence.dataset:                "Research datasets and their DOIs."

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ROLE REQUIREMENTS — how the CONTRACT decides which roles the plan needs. Each rule matches DATA
# tokens against the plan's ROUTING TEXT (question + review_subject + method_designs + unit_levels +
# core_concept aliases + framing devices + genre + geographies — assembled by source_router.py). The
# tokens are FIELD-NATIVE vocabulary a real contract in that field prints; the matcher is generic and
# lives in code, the vocabulary lives here. `default: true` = always required (the OA-first backbone).
# `any_of` = required if the routing text contains ANY listed token as a whole word/phrase.
#
# OVER-FIRING IS SAFE (we look in one more place); UNDER-FIRING is the danger. So triggers are
# INCLUSIVE. The one hard invariant the demo checks: a clinical plan must NOT acquire the economics
# working-paper role — so `evidence.working_paper.economics` is triggered ONLY by economics-native
# terms, none of which a clinical or legal question carries.
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role_requirements:
  - role: discover.index
    default: true
  - role: resolve.identity
    default: true
  - role: resolve.locations
    default: true
  - role: fetch.oa_fulltext
    default: true

  - role: evidence.peer_reviewed
    # Any literature-review / scholarly-synthesis artifact, or an explicit journal/peer-reviewed policy.
    any_of: ["literature review", "systematic review", "scholarly", "peer-reviewed", "peer reviewed",
             "journal article", "journal articles", "meta-analysis", "meta analysis", "review the literature",
             "academic literature", "state of the art", "research synthesis"]

  - role: evidence.working_paper.economics
    any_of: ["labor market", "labour market", "labor economics", "wage", "wages", "employment",
             "unemployment", "gdp", "productivity", "inflation", "monetary policy", "fiscal",
             "trade", "tariff", "econometric", "difference-in-differences", "instrumental variable",
             "quasi-experimental", "elasticity", "occupation", "occupations", "firm-level",
             "labor supply", "labor demand", "economic growth", "nber", "iza"]

  - role: evidence.preprint.general
    any_of: ["machine learning", "deep learning", "neural network", "neural networks", "algorithm",
             "algorithms", "transformer", "large language model", "reinforcement learning", "quantum",
             "astrophysics", "condensed matter", "theorem", "benchmark", "computer vision",
             "natural language processing", "artificial intelligence", "generative ai", "gpu"]

  - role: evidence.preprint.biomedical
    any_of: ["clinical", "clinical trial", "randomized", "randomised", "rct", "placebo", "efficacy",
             "adverse event", "adverse events", "mortality", "morbidity", "cohort", "in vitro",
             "in vivo", "patients", "patient", "dose", "dosing", "therapy", "therapeutic", "disease",
             "diagnosis", "biomarker", "hazard ratio", "epidemiolog", "incidence", "prevalence",
             "comorbidit", "pathophysiolog"]

  - role: evidence.working_paper.social_legal
    any_of: ["case law", "statute", "statutes", "statutory", "regulation", "regulations", "regulatory",
             "court", "courts", "judicial", "holding", "liability", "plaintiff", "defendant", "tort",

106	# THE IMMUTABLE BLOB STORE

108	
109	class BlobStore:
110	    """Content-addressed, write-once. `put` is idempotent BY CONSTRUCTION, not by convention."""
111	
112	    def __init__(self, root: Path | str | None = None):

116	        self.root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

118	    def _path(self, digest: str) -> Path:

121	    def put(self, raw: bytes) -> tuple[str, str]:
122	        """-> (blob_id, sha256). Writing the same bytes twice writes the same blob, always."""
123	        digest = hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest()
124	        p = self._path(digest)
125	        if not p.exists():

230	# THE LANE RECORDS (Sol V9 §1) — AND THE LINEAGE THAT MAKES ROUTE CREDIT MEAN ANYTHING
231	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
232	"""

234	
235	`source_router.classify_discovery_outcome(ledger, unit, adapter)` checked the TRANSPORT outcome per
236	adapter — correctly — and then asked what we HELD by reducing over EVERY manifestation of the unit. But
237	a manifestation's `adapter` is the CONTENT HOST it was pulled from (`content:arxiv.org`), not the
238	resolver that proposed it. So the reduction had no way to tell whose candidate those bytes came from:
239	if Unpaywall proposed the URL that produced the PDF, and CORE merely answered "RESPONDED" to a search
240	that found nothing, CORE's discovery outcome read FETCHED. A ladder built on that number measures
241	nothing — every route inherits the union of every other route's luck, and "unique incremental yield"
242	becomes an arithmetic identity.
243	
244	THE FIX IS A CHAIN, NOT A FLAG:
245	
246	    resolver request  ->  candidate  ->  content request  ->  redirects  ->  manifestation
247	    (request_id)          (candidate_id)   (request_id, candidate_id)         (candidate_id)
248	
249	`candidate_id` is minted where the candidate is BORN — inside the adapter that proposed the URL — and it
250	is carried, unchanged, through the content request and onto the manifestation. A reducer can then ask
251	the only question that means anything: "which manifestations descend from a candidate THIS ADAPTER
252	proposed?" A route that proposed nothing gets credited with nothing, however many other routes succeeded.
253	
254	THE ADAPTER STILL MAY NOT WRITE A CONCLUSION. Sol V9 §1: "The adapter must not write FULLTEXT, THIS_WORK,
255	VERSION_OF_RECORD, ADMISSIBLE, or an expression edge." Those are REDUCER outputs. `version_hint` and
256	`license_observation` below are named `_hint` / `_observation` for exactly that reason: they are what a
257	repository SAID, kept because it is evidence, and they are never read as what a document IS.
258	"""
259	
260	
261	@dataclass(frozen=True)
262	class ResolveContext:
263	    """WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR, and what the contract will and will not accept. Sol V9 §1.
264	
265	    The router's input is this — STRUCTURED CAPABILITIES — never a lexical guess at the topic.
266	    """
267	    work_id: str
268	    contract_id: str = ''
269	    identifiers: tuple[str, ...] = ()          # DOI, PMCID, PMID, OAI ID, CELEX, ECLI, NCT...
270	    title: str = ''
271	    authors: tuple[str, ...] = ()
272	    year: int | None = None
273	    required_artifact_kinds: tuple[str, ...] = ()
274	    permitted_expression_kinds: tuple[str, ...] = ()
275	    required_capabilities: tuple[str, ...] = ()
276	
277	
278	@dataclass(frozen=True)
279	class DocumentCandidate:
280	    """A URL AN ADAPTER PROPOSED. NOT a document, not a version, not evidence — a lead with a lineage.
281	
282	    `version_hint` and `license_observation` are OBSERVATIONS ONLY, and the suffixes are load-bearing:
283	    `acceptedVersion` from a repository is a string a repository emitted, and the entire V9 P0 was the
284	    project of treating that string as a fact about bytes. It is recorded and it concludes NOTHING.
285	    """
286	    candidate_id: str
287	    work_id: str
288	    discovered_by_route: str                   # the ADAPTER that proposed it. The lineage root.
289	    resolver_request_id: str = ''              # the request in which it was proposed
290	    identifier_used: str = ''
291	    retrieval_url: str = ''
292	    repository_record_url: str = ''
293	    media_hint: str = ''
294	    version_hint: str = ''                     # observation only — NEVER a version decision
295	    license_observation: str = ''              # observation only
296	    raw_metadata_blob_id: str = ''
297	    raw_metadata_hash: str = ''
298	
299	
300	def make_candidate_id(unit: str, adapter: str, url: str) -> str:
301	    """DERIVED, never minted. The same adapter proposing the same URL for the same work is the same
302	    candidate — so a re-run does not fork the lineage, and two adapters proposing the SAME url are two
303	    DIFFERENT candidates, which is exactly right: they are two routes, and each must be scored alone."""
304	    h = hashlib.sha256(f'{unit}|{adapter}|{url}'.encode()).hexdigest()
305	    return f'cand:{h[:16]}'
306	
307	
308	def open_ledger(path: Path | str | None = None) -> Ledger:
309	    """THE ONE DURABLE LEDGER, opened AS IT STANDS ON DISK. Every fetcher in a run shares it.
310	
311	    `Ledger.load` re-reads the history, so a fetcher started after another fetcher finished REDUCES
312	    OVER WHAT HAPPENED, not over the handful of events it emitted itself.
313	    """
314	    return Ledger.load(Path(path) if path else LEDGER_PATH)
315	
316	
317	def content_host(url: str) -> str:
318	    """The adapter name for a DOCUMENT fetch. A content host is a backend too: if nber.org 403s us,
319	    we were BLOCKED BY NBER, and that is a fact worth keeping — distinct from "no copy exists"."""
320	    try:
321	        return 'content:' + (urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc or 'unknown')
322	    except Exception:
323	        return 'content:unknown'
324	
325	
326	class Acquirer:
327	    """Emits observations. Cannot emit a status: no method here takes one.
328	
329	    Every method is a thin, boring wrapper over `Ledger.emit`, and that is the point — the interesting
330	    part of this class is what it DOES NOT EXPOSE.
331	    """
332	
333	    def __init__(self, actor: str, ledger: Ledger | None = None, blobs: BlobStore | None = None):
334	        self.actor = actor
335	        self.ledger = ledger if ledger is not None else open_ledger()
336	        self.blobs = blobs if blobs is not None else BlobStore()
337	
338	    # -- 1. ROUTE_PLANNED -------------------------------------------------------------------------
339	    def plan_route(self, unit: str, adapters: Iterable[str], **obs) -> None:
340	        """`route_complete` will mean EVERY ONE OF THESE HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
341	
342	        It must never mean "an adapter was mapped", so this is called BEFORE the loop, with the
343	        adapters we are ABOUT to try — not after, with the ones that happened to answer.
344	        """
345	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, self.actor,
346	                         adapters=list(adapters), **obs)
347	
348	    # -- 2 & 3. THE EXCEPTION BOUNDARY ------------------------------------------------------------
349	    def get(self, unit: str, adapter: str, url: str, *, tries: int = 4, timeout: int = 30,
350	            candidate_id: str = '', **obs) -> Response:
351	        """One logical request. BACKEND_ATTEMPTED before each attempt; EXACTLY ONE of
352	        RESPONSE_RECEIVED | THROTTLED | BLOCKED at the exception boundary of each attempt.
353	
354	        THE RETRIES SHARE A `request_id`. That is load-bearing: a 429 that a backoff RESOLVED and a
355	        429 that we GAVE UP ON are different facts, and the reducer must be able to tell them apart
356	        without either of them being deleted from the log. Both are here, forever; the reducer reads
357	        the request's FINAL terminal event.
358	
359	          exhausted 429s -> the last terminal event is THROTTLED -> BACKEND_FAILED. FOREVER.
360	          429 then 200   -> the last terminal event is RESPONSE_RECEIVED -> RESPONDED. The 429 is
361	                            still on disk, because the backoff working is not the same as it never
362	                            having been throttled.
363	        """
364	        rid = f'{adapter}#{next(_RID)}'
365	        last: Response | None = None
366	        for attempt in range(1, max(1, tries) + 1):
367	            self._space(url)
368	            self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, self.actor,
369	                             adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt, **obs)
370	            try:
371	                req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=UA)
372	                # NOTE: `urllib.request.urlopen` is resolved AT CALL TIME, on purpose. The adversary
373	                # harness monkeypatches it to force a 429 storm, and a `from ... import urlopen` would
374	                # bind past the patch and test nothing.
375	                with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as r:
376	                    raw = r.read()
377	                    ctype = r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') or ''
378	                    code = int(getattr(r, 'status', 200) or 200)
379	                self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
380	                                 adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt,
381	                                 http_status=code, content_type=ctype, n_bytes=len(raw))
382	                return Response(RESPONDED, code, raw, ctype, url, adapter, rid)
383	
384	            except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
385	                code = int(getattr(e, 'code', 0) or 0)
386	
387	                if code in THROTTLE_CODES:
388	                    # ** A 429 PERSISTS AS THROTTLED. IT CANNOT BECOME CITATION_ONLY. **
389	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.THROTTLED, self.actor,
390	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt,
391	                                     http_status=code, retry_after=self._retry_after(e))
392	                    last = Response(THROTTLED, code, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, f'HTTP {code}')
393	                    if attempt < tries:
394	                        self._backoff(attempt)
395	                        continue
396	                    return last
397	
398	                if code in BLOCK_CODES:
399	                    # A fact about ENTITLEMENT. Retrying a paywall is not politeness, it is noise.
400	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BLOCKED, self.actor,
401	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt,
402	                                     http_status=code)
403	                    return Response(BLOCKED, code, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, f'HTTP {code}')
404	
405	                if code == 404:
406	                    # THE BACKEND ANSWERED. "My index does not have this" is a RESPONSE, and it is a
407	                    # fact about THEIR INDEX — not about whether the paper is free somewhere else.
408	                    # (Semantic Scholar 404s the QJE DOI for Autor/Levy/Murnane. NBER WP 8337 exists.)
409	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
410	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt,
411	                                     http_status=404, n_bytes=0)
412	                    return Response(NOT_INDEXED, 404, b'', '', url, adapter, rid)
413	
414	                # 5xx and the rest: they responded, and the response is not an answer.
415	                self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
416	                                 adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt,
417	                                 http_status=code, transport_error=f'HTTP {code}')
418	                last = Response(TRANSPORT_ERROR, code, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, f'HTTP {code}')
419	                if code >= 500 and attempt < tries:
420	                    self._backoff(attempt)
421	                    continue
422	                return last
423	
424	            except Exception as e:
425	                # A HANG IS NOT A GAP. `transport_error` carries the exception CLASS (a bounded,
426	                # component-authored word); the message goes under `reason_text`, which the ledger's
427	                # conclusion guard treats as raw text from the world — because an exception string is
428	                # free text and could otherwise smuggle "not available" into the log as a conclusion.
429	                self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
430	                                 adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt,
431	                                 transport_error=type(e).__name__, reason_text=str(e)[:200])
432	                last = Response(TRANSPORT_ERROR, None, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, type(e).__name__)
433	                if attempt < tries:
434	                    self._backoff(attempt)
435	                    continue
436	                return last
437	
438	        return last or Response(TRANSPORT_ERROR, None, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, 'no attempt made')
439	
440	    def get_json(self, unit: str, adapter: str, url: str, **kw) -> tuple[Response, Any | None]:
441	        r = self.get(unit, adapter, url, **kw)
442	        return r, r.json()
443	
444	    # -- 4. CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED ------------------------------------------------------------------
445	    def candidate(self, unit: str, adapter: str, url: str, *,
446	                  resolver_request_id: str = '', **obs) -> str:
447	        """An adapter returned a URL. It is a CANDIDATE — not evidence, not a copy of anything yet.
448	
449	        RETURNS THE `candidate_id`, AND THE CALLER MUST CARRY IT. That id is the root of the lineage
450	
451	            resolver request -> candidate -> content request -> redirects -> manifestation
452	
453	        and it is the ONLY thing that can say WHICH ROUTE a document is owed to. Without it the
454	        discovery reducer had to guess, and it guessed by reducing over every manifestation of the work —
455	        so a route that found nothing was credited with a route that found everything. A ladder measured
456	        with that reducer cannot see incremental yield at all.
457	        """
458	        cid = make_candidate_id(unit, adapter, url)
459	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED, self.actor,
460	                         adapter=adapter, url=url, candidate_id=cid,
461	                         resolver_request_id=resolver_request_id, **obs)
462	        return cid
463	
464	    # -- 5 & 6. MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, then CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED -------------------------------
465	    def record_manifestation(self, unit: str, *, locator: str, raw: bytes, text: str,
466	                             adapter: str, candidate_id: str = '', requested_title: str = '',
467	                             requested_authors: Iterable[str] | None = None,
468	                             requested_doi: str = '', requested_venue: str = '',
469	                             requested_year: int | None = None, source_type: str = '',
470	                             extraction_method: str = '', **obs) -> dict:
471	        """The bytes we hold, pinned to an IMMUTABLE BLOB and its HASH — then profiled by the SHARED
472	        reducer, which is the only thing allowed to look at content.
473	
474	        NOTE WHAT THIS SIGNATURE DOES NOT ACCEPT: a content status, a fulltext_source, a word count, a
475	        `complete` flag, or a profile. The profile is computed HERE, by `observe_text`, from the bytes
476	        that were just stored. A fetcher cannot hand one in, so a fetcher cannot inflate one — which is
477	        exactly how `fulltext_words: 8,000` came to sit beside 535 words of cookie banner.
478	        """
479	        blob_id, byte_sha = self.blobs.put(raw or b'')
480	        text_blob_id, text_sha = self.blobs.put_text(text or '')
481	
482	        self.ledger.emit(
483	            unit, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, self.actor,
484	            adapter=adapter,
485	            # THE LINEAGE (Sol V9 §1). WHICH CANDIDATE, AND THEREFORE WHICH ROUTE, THESE BYTES ARE OWED
486	            # TO. Empty means UNATTRIBUTED — and an unattributed manifestation credits NO route, which
487	            # is the honest answer and, importantly, the SAFE default: the old reducer credited EVERY
488	            # route instead.
489	            candidate_id=candidate_id or '',
490	            locator=locator,                 # THE URL THESE BYTES CAME FROM. wp_fetch never recorded
491	            #                                # it, so `oa_url` (the PUBLISHER's link, left by an
492	            #                                # earlier fetcher) was the only URL on the row — a locator
493	            #                                # naming aeaweb.org over the bytes of an NBER working paper.
494	            blob_id=blob_id, byte_sha256=byte_sha, n_bytes=len(raw or b''),
495	            text_blob_id=text_blob_id, text_sha256=text_sha,
496	            extraction_method=extraction_method,
497	            # THE IDENTITY WE ASKED FOR — kept beside the bytes, so the reducer can ask whether the
498	            # bytes are the work we requested. (A title search for "Rise of the Machines" returned
499	            # Yang-Hui He's arXiv paper on theorem-proving, and it was filed under an HR journal
500	            # article by Parry et al. Nothing on the row could have caught that; the requested identity
501	            # is what makes it catchable.)
502	            requested_title=requested_title or '',
503	            requested_authors=list(requested_authors or []),
504	            requested_doi=requested_doi or '',
505	            requested_venue=requested_venue or '',
506	            requested_year=requested_year,
507	            source_type=source_type or '',
508	            **obs)
509	
510	        # ---- 6. THE SHARED ARTIFACT-PROFILE REDUCER. The caller supplies no part of this. ---------
511	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observe_text',
512	                         **observe_text(text or ''))
513	
514	        return dict(blob_id=blob_id, byte_sha256=byte_sha,
515	                    text_blob_id=text_blob_id, text_sha256=text_sha)
516	
517	    # -- a budget stop IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP -----------------------------------------------------
518	    def budget_stopped(self, unit: str, **obs) -> None:
519	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BUDGET_STOPPED, self.actor, **obs)
520	
521	    # -- politeness ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
522	    @staticmethod
523	    def _retry_after(e: urllib.error.HTTPError) -> str:
524	        try:
525	            return str(e.headers.get('Retry-After') or '') if e.headers else ''
526	        except Exception:
527	            return ''
528	
529	    @staticmethod
530	    def _space(url: str) -> None:
531	        host = urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc or 'unknown'
532	        wait = _HOST_LAST.get(host, 0.0) + SPACING_S - time.time()
533	        if wait > 0:
534	            time.sleep(wait)
535	        _HOST_LAST[host] = time.time()
536	
537	    @staticmethod
538	    def _backoff(attempt: int) -> None:
539	        d = 3.0 * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) * BACKOFF_SCALE
540	        if d > 0:
541	            time.sleep(d)
542	
543	
544	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
545	# WHAT DO WE ALREADY HOLD? — DERIVED from the bytes, never read off a label
546	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
547	
548	def held_content_class(row: dict) -> tuple[str, dict]:
549	    """The content class the ROW'S OWN BYTES earn, through the shared reducer.
550	
551	    THIS REPLACES `row['content_status'] == 'CITATION_ONLY'` AS THE TARGET SELECTOR, and the
552	    replacement is not cosmetic. Selecting targets by the label meant:
553	
554	      * deep_fetch NEVER RETRIED the 535-word aeaweb cookie banner, because the label on it said
555	        FULLTEXT. The label was written by the fetcher that failed to get the paper.
556	      * wp_fetch NEVER RETRIED the paper whose PDF decoded to pure (cid:NN) glyph codes, for the
557	        same reason.
558	
559	    A selector that reads a component's own claim about its own success can only ever confirm it.
560	    """
561	    L = Ledger()                                    # scratch, in-memory: this derives, it does not log
562	    unit = row.get('doi') or row.get('title') or '?'
563	    text = (row.get('fulltext') or '').strip()
564	    if text:
565	        L.emit(unit, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'corpus',
566	               locator=row.get('oa_url') or '',
567	               requested_title=row.get('title') or '',
568	               requested_authors=list(row.get('authors') or []),
569	               source_type=str(row.get('type') or ''))
570	        L.emit(unit, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observe_text', **observe_text(text))
571	    return derive_content_profile(L.events(unit))
572	
573	
574	def holds_usable_document(row: dict) -> bool:
575	    """Do we hold a COMPLETE document for this row? (not: does a label say so)"""
576	    return held_content_class(row)[0] == C_FULLTEXT
577	
578	
579	def holds_nothing(row: dict) -> bool:
580	    """Do we hold no usable document at all — no bytes, or bytes that are not a document?
581	
582	    C_NOT_DOC (a cookie banner) and C_UNREADABLE (a PDF whose font never decoded) belong HERE, with
583	    C_CITATION. Under the old label they were `FULLTEXT` and were never fetched again.
584	    """
585	    cls, _ = held_content_class(row)
586	    if cls in (C_NOT_DOC, C_UNREADABLE):
587	        return True
588	    return cls == C_CITATION and not (row.get('abstract') or '').strip()
589	
590	
591	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
592	# MERGING EVENT STREAMS — an append-only log is merged by UNION, never by a winner
593	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
594	
595	def _event_identity(d: dict) -> str:
596	    """What makes two records THE SAME OBSERVATION. Not `seq` — that is local to one log."""
597	    return json.dumps([d.get('unit'), d.get('kind'), d.get('actor'), round(float(d.get('ts') or 0), 4),
598	                       d.get('payload')], sort_keys=True)
599	
600	
601	def merge_ledgers(paths: Iterable[Path | str], out: Path | str) -> tuple[int, int]:
602	    """Union of append-only logs, re-sequenced. -> (n_events, n_duplicates_dropped).
603	
604	    Two fetchers running against one file made the LAST WRITER WIN and silently flatten the other's
605	    work. Two fetchers appending to one LOG cannot: an observation is not a slot to be overwritten,
606	    it is a record that happened. The only merge an append-only log admits is a union.
607	    """
608	    seen: set[str] = set()
609	    rows: list[dict] = []
610	    dupes = 0
611	    for p in paths:
612	        p = Path(p)
613	        if not p.exists():
614	            continue
615	        for line in p.read_text().splitlines():
616	            line = line.strip()
617	            if not line:
618	                continue
619	            d = json.loads(line)
620	            key = _event_identity(d)
621	            if key in seen:
622	                dupes += 1
623	                continue
624	            seen.add(key)
625	            rows.append(d)
626	    rows.sort(key=lambda d: (float(d.get('ts') or 0), int(d.get('seq') or 0)))
627	    out = Path(out)
628	    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
629	    with open(out, 'w') as fh:
630	        for i, d in enumerate(rows, 1):
631	            d['seq'] = i                            # re-sequenced: seq is an ORDER, not an identity
632	            fh.write(json.dumps(d, sort_keys=True) + '\n')
633	    return len(rows), dupes
634	
635	
636	def manifestations_of(ledger: Ledger, unit: str) -> list[dict]:
637	    """Every distinct set of bytes we have EVER held for this work, newest last. NOTHING IS DELETED.
638	
639	    Deduped on `text_sha256` — the content address. Fetching the same document twice is one
640	    manifestation; fetching two DIFFERENT documents for one DOI is two, and the graph keeps both,
641	    because "which of these is the paper" is a question for a reducer with evidence, not for whichever
642	    fetcher wrote the file last.
643	    """
644	    out: dict[str, dict] = {}
645	    for e in ledger.events(unit, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED):
646	        if 'derived_by' in e.payload:
647	            continue
648	        h = e.payload.get('text_sha256') or e.payload.get('byte_sha256')
649	        if not h:
650	            continue
651	        out[h] = {**e.payload, 'seq': e.seq, 'actor': e.actor}
652	    return list(out.values())
653	
654	
655	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
656	# THE EVIDENCE SELECTION REDUCER — which of the bytes we hold is THE DOCUMENT?
657	#
658	# THE OLD ANSWER WAS "THE LONGEST ONE" (merge_corpus.py:47-54, and a `if len(t.split()) >= 500: break`
659	# in every fetcher). Length is not a property that distinguishes a paper from a cookie banner: the
660	# aeaweb.org landing page for Autor (2015) is 535 words and the ORA landing page for Frey & Osborne is
661	# 548, and BOTH of them beat a real 400-word abstract on length while being no document at all.
662	#
663	# The answer here is: the one the SHARED REDUCER says is a complete, finding-bearing document — and
664	# among those, the most AUTHORITATIVE VERSION, which is a question about peer review, not about size.
665	# Length breaks ties only between documents already agreed to be the same kind of thing.
666	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
667	
668	#: How much authority a version carries. `journal_version` outranks `working_paper` because PEER REVIEW
669	#: CHANGES NUMBERS — Acemoglu-Restrepo robots-and-jobs is 0.37pp in NBER WP 23285 and 0.2pp in the
670	#: published JPE. It is not a preference. They are different documents with different numbers in them.
671	_VERSION_AUTHORITY = {
672	    'journal_version': 6, 'official_text': 6, 'registry_record': 6,
673	    'proceedings_version': 5, 'accepted_manuscript': 4,
674	    'working_paper': 3, 'preprint': 2, 'unknown': 1,
675	}
676	
677	#: The content classes that can be a document at all, best first.
678	_CLASS_RANK = {C_FULLTEXT: 3, C_ABSTRACT: 2, C_CITATION: 1, C_NOT_DOC: 0, C_UNREADABLE: 0}
679	
680	
681	def classify_manifestation(m: dict, blobs: BlobStore) -> dict:
682	    """Run ONE manifestation's bytes back through the shared reducer. Concludes nothing on its own."""
683	    from provenance import derive_expression_kind      # local: provenance imports are not free
684	
685	    text = blobs.get_text(m['text_blob_id']) if m.get('text_blob_id') else ''
686	    L = Ledger()
687	    L.emit('m', EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'select', **{
688	        k: v for k, v in m.items()
689	        if k in ('locator', 'requested_title', 'requested_authors', 'source_type', 'adapter')})
690	    L.emit('m', EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observe_text', **observe_text(text))
691	    cls, info = derive_content_profile(L.events('m'))
692	    ekind, ebasis = derive_expression_kind(text)
693	    return dict(
694	        manifestation=m, text=text, content_class=cls, artifact_kind=info.get('artifact_kind'),
695	        is_complete=bool(info.get('complete')), expression_kind=ekind, expression_basis=ebasis,
696	        readable_words=info.get('readable_word_count', 0) or 0,
697	        basis=info.get('reason', ''),
698	        rank=(_CLASS_RANK.get(cls, 0), _VERSION_AUTHORITY.get(ekind, 0),
699	              info.get('readable_word_count', 0) or 0))
700	
701	
702	def select_evidence(ledger: Ledger, unit: str, blobs: BlobStore | None = None) -> dict | None:
703	    """Which manifestation is THE DOCUMENT for this work? -> the winner, with its basis. Or None.
704	
705	    EVERY CANDIDATE SURVIVES THIS CALL. Selection is a VIEW, not a deletion: the losers keep their
706	    blobs, their hashes and their events, and the provenance graph carries every one of them. "Which
707	    of these is the paper" is a question a reducer answers with evidence, and it is allowed to be
708	    answered differently tomorrow, on the same bytes, by a better reducer.
709	    """
710	    blobs = blobs or BlobStore()
711	    cands = [classify_manifestation(m, blobs) for m in manifestations_of(ledger, unit)]
712	    if not cands:
713	        return None
714	    return max(cands, key=lambda c: c['rank'])
715	
716	
717	if __name__ == '__main__':
718	    print(__doc__)
719	    print('This module is a library. Its invariants are tested by:  '
720	          'python3 scripts/test_acquisition_observes.py')
721

84	## 2. Route specifications
85	
86	All “yield” figures are gross forecasts among the approximately 2,490 no-known-OA candidates. They overlap and are not additive.
87	
88	### PMC
89	
90	- Query:
91	  1. Convert DOI/PMID to PMCID with the PMC ID Converter.
92	  2. `GetRecord` from PMC OAI using `oai:pubmedcentral.nih.gov:<numeric-pmcid>` and `metadataPrefix=pmc`.
93	  3. If needed, query the PMC OA service for PDF/tarball links.
94	- Resolves by: PMCID first; DOI or PMID through ID conversion.
95	- Returns: JATS full-text XML where rights permit; the OA service may return PDF/package links. A PMCID does not guarantee downloadable OA full text.
96	- Politeness: PMC OAI allows at most three requests per second, no concurrent requests, and recommends large harvesting off-peak. Batch up to 200 IDs through the ID converter. Register tool/email. [PMC ID Converter](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/id-converter-api/), [PMC OAI](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/oai/), [PMC OA service](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/oa-service/), [NCBI usage guidance](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK25497/)
97	- Clinical: primary full-text route; prefer JATS because identity, sections, contributors, DOI, and closure are structurally observable.
98	- Legal/comparative: invoked only when the contract requests scholarly literature carrying DOI/PMID identifiers, not for official legal texts.
99	- Thin evidence: a rights-blocked or missing OAI record is a route observation, not proof that no document exists.
100	- Data edit: PMC endpoints, identifier transforms, metadata prefixes, and rate budget. Code changes only for a new protocol/parser primitive.
101	- Silent failures: PMCID exists but full text is not in the OA subset; XML is front matter only; NIH manuscript is mistaken for publisher VoR; embargo/live status is ignored.
102	- Gross yield forecast: 20–60.
103	
104	### Europe PMC
105	
106	- Query: exact DOI search using `/rest/search?query=DOI:"..."&resultType=core&format=json`; inspect `pmcid`, `fullTextIdList`, `fullTextUrlList`, `hasPDF`, and OA fields. Fetch JATS with `/rest/{PMCID}/fullTextXML`.
107	- Resolves by: DOI, PMCID, PMID, or Europe PMC source/id.
108	- Returns: core metadata, full-text URLs, and OA JATS XML for eligible records. The currently configured `/{source}/{id}/fullTextXML` shape is wrong for PMCID retrieval.
109	- Politeness: official documentation does not publish a stable numeric public quota. Start at one request/second and one in flight, then obey `429`, `Retry-After`, and any returned limit headers. [Europe PMC REST](https://europepmc.org/RestfulWebService), [official API specification](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/api/swagger.json)
110	- Clinical: complements PMC with European indexing and identifiers.
111	- Legal/comparative: only for relevant scholarly literature.
112	- Thin evidence: distinguishes “record found, no accessible full text” from “identifier not found.”
113	- Data edit: endpoint templates, response selectors, rate budget.
114	- Silent failures: abstract mistaken for full text; PMCID present but XML unavailable; source/id normalization error; duplicated PMC copy counted as incremental.
115	- Incremental gross yield forecast after PMC: 5–20.
116	
117	### DOAJ
118	
119	- Query: exact article search on DOI; read `bibjson.link[]` entries whose type is `fulltext`.
120	- Resolves by: DOI primarily; title plus author only as a candidate generator.
121	- Returns: metadata and publisher/repository full-text URLs, generally not document bytes.
122	- Politeness: average two requests/second; brief bursts may be queued. [DOAJ API](https://doaj.org/api/)
123	- Clinical: finds OA journals outside PMC coverage.
124	- Legal/comparative: finds OA legal and comparative scholarship, but never official legislation or judgments.
125	- Thin evidence: a DOAJ miss means “not indexed in DOAJ,” not “not open.”
126	- Data edit: query template and link selector.
127	- Silent failures: stale or landing-page URL; URL redirects to login; DOI metadata points to a supplement; directory membership is mistaken for byte-level version proof.
128	- Incremental gross yield forecast: 10–25.
129	
130	### CORE
131	
132	- Query: `/v3/search/works?q=doi:"..."`; prefer record `downloadUrl` or documented output download. Treat a returned `fullText` field as a derived text manifestation, not automatically as a complete PDF equivalent.
133	- Resolves by: DOI; CORE work/output ID; OAI ID; title plus author for unresolved candidates.
134	- Returns: metadata, extracted full text, source URLs, download URLs, and repository/OAI identifiers.
135	- Politeness: enforce the authenticated tier reported by CORE and its rate headers. Current documentation describes materially different daily and per-minute budgets by tier; full-text download is more expensive. The currently configured credential returns 401, so preflight must mark the route unavailable rather than conclude that content is absent. [CORE API](https://api.core.ac.uk/docs/v3)
136	- Clinical: useful for accepted manuscripts and institutional copies outside PMC.
137	- Legal/comparative: strong for repository scholarship and working papers, but repository copies are not official legal texts.
138	- Thin evidence: an exhausted authenticated exact-DOI query contributes to search coverage; an unauthenticated or 401 run contributes only `BACKEND_FAILED`.
139	- Data edit: credential profile, query template, output selectors, rate costs.
140	- Silent failures: OCR/truncated `fullText`; supplement returned as primary file; API tier omits full text; direct file link expires; repository metadata says accepted manuscript but bytes are another work.
141	- Gross yield forecast: 60–140.
142	
143	### OpenAIRE Graph
144	
145	- Query: current Graph API `/graph/v3/research-products?pid=<doi>`; inspect product instances, PIDs, access rights, licences, and URLs.
146	- Resolves by: DOI/PID or OpenAIRE research-product ID.
147	- Returns: graph metadata and candidate locations. URLs remain candidates; OpenAIRE metadata is not document proof.
148	- Politeness: current documented limits are up to 60 requests/hour unauthenticated and 7,200/hour authenticated. Use authentication and schedule against the lower of configured and observed limits. [OpenAIRE Graph API](https://graph.openaire.eu/docs/apis/graph-api/), [OpenAIRE API terms](https://graph.openaire.eu/docs/apis/terms)
149	- Clinical: finds institutional and national repository copies not exposed through PMC.
150	- Legal/comparative: finds cross-jurisdictional scholarship and repository deposits.
151	- Thin evidence: exact PID exhaustion can close this route; stale or inaccessible candidate URLs cannot.
152	- Data edit: replace the legacy endpoint in YAML with the Graph v3 operation and response selectors.
153	- Silent failures: access-right label is stale; URL is only a repository landing page; multiple instances represent the same file; graph metadata and fetched bytes disagree.
154	- Gross yield forecast: 45–110.
155	
156	### Zenodo
157	
158	- Query: `/api/records?q=doi:"..."`; also inspect `pids`, related identifiers, `conceptdoi`, version, resource type, and each file.
159	- Resolves by: record DOI, concept DOI, related DOI, Zenodo record ID; title plus creator only for candidate generation.
160	- Returns: record metadata and direct file links.
161	- Politeness: rate limits are endpoint/account dependent; use `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `Remaining`, and `Reset`, and defer on 429. [Zenodo API](https://developers.zenodo.org/)
162	- Clinical: can contain manuscripts, trial artifacts, protocols, data, and supplements. Artifact type must remain explicit.
163	- Legal/comparative: may contain scholarship or datasets, but not authoritative law merely because it has a DOI.
164	- Thin evidence: non-article files may themselves be the correct evidence artifact if the contract requested protocols or data; otherwise they do not fill the full-article role.
165	- Data edit: resource-type mappings, PID fields, file selectors, rate budget.
166	- Silent failures: dataset/supplement mistaken for article; concept DOI resolves to the wrong version; restricted record has metadata but no bytes; multiple files are concatenated into a fictitious document.
167	- Gross yield forecast: 5–20.
168	
169	### Institutional OAI-PMH
170	
171	- Query:
172	  - Do not attempt global per-work OAI harvesting.
173	  - Obtain the OAI identifier and repository base URL through CORE, OpenAIRE, repository metadata, or an incrementally maintained local index.
174	  - Use `GetRecord(identifier, metadataPrefix)`.
175	  - Rich formats such as JATS, METS, MODS, OAI-ORE, or repository-specific XML may expose document URLs. `oai_dc` commonly exposes metadata or a landing page only.
176	- Resolves by: OAI identifier. OAI-PMH has no universal exact-DOI lookup.
177	- Returns: metadata records; sometimes file URLs or embedded structured full text, depending on the repository and metadata format.
178	- Politeness: repository-specific. Default to one in flight and a two-second interval until the repository row states otherwise; obey `Retry-After`, robots rules, resumption tokens, and deletion records. [OAI-PMH specification](https://www.openarchives.org/OAI/openarchivesprotocol.html)
179	- Clinical: reaches university manuscripts and subject repositories beyond PMC.
180	- Legal/comparative: reaches institutional scholarship; official courts and legislatures should use dedicated official-source adapters.
181	- Thin evidence: only a clean `GetRecord`/indexed lookup closes that repository. A missing local DOI-to-OAI mapping does not.
182	- Data edit: each repository is a row containing base URL, supported metadata prefixes, identifier mappings, file XPath/selectors, and rate policy. A code edit is needed only for a genuinely new metadata dialect primitive.
183	- Silent failures: `oai_dc` landing page treated as PDF; deleted record treated as no-world evidence; DOI belongs to a cited reference; repository cover sheet hides a different article; resumption state is lost.
184	- Gross yield forecast: 35–90.
185	
186	## 3. Priority and stopping policy
187	
188	Given a DOI, resolve in waves:
189	
190	1. Re-derive identity, completeness, and admissibility for already held bytes.
191	2. In parallel, perform cheap exact-ID resolution through PMC/Europe PMC where applicable, DOAJ, Unpaywall, OpenAlex, and Semantic Scholar.
192	3. Resolve through CORE, OpenAIRE, and Zenodo.
193	4. Use resulting OAI IDs/repository locations for targeted institutional OAI-PMH.
194	5. Use title-plus-author only after exact identifiers fail. It may generate candidates but cannot establish identity.
195	
196	Candidate fetching is ordered separately:
197	
198	1. Official or authoritative structured full text permitted by the contract: PMC JATS, official legal XML, registry JSON.
199	2. Publisher-hosted OA VoR.
200	3. Repository-hosted bytes whose own front matter proves they are the VoR.
201	4. Accepted manuscript.
202	5. Preprint or working paper.
203	
204	A publisher URL gets one normal attempt. A 403 ends that URL attempt and advances to repositories; it does not trigger repeated publisher requests.
205	
206	Stop rules:
207	
208	- If a complete, identity-confirmed, policy-admissible manifestation is obtained, remaining lower-priority work is cancelled with a pointer to the reducer decision that satisfied the contract.
209	- If only an accepted manuscript is found under a journal-VoR-only contract, keep searching every applicable VoR route.
210	- If the contract permits accepted manuscripts, it can satisfy the role only as an accepted manuscript and must be attributed as such.
211	
212	## 4. Version equivalence
213	
214	`accepted_manuscript_of` remains a useful bibliographic edge, but it is not span-preserving.
215	
216	Change the attribution API from manifestation-wide permission to binding-specific permission:
217	
218	```text
219	resolve_attribution(binding_id, attribution_policy)
220	```
221	
222	A `SpanCorrespondence` must contain:
223	
224	```text
225	source_manifestation_id + raw/text hash + offsets + verbatim span
226	target_manifestation_id + raw/text hash + offsets + verbatim span
227	canonicalization algorithm and version
228	exact canonical-span hash
229	identity decisions for both manifestations
230	```
231	
232	The verifier rechecks both hashes, both offsets, and exact canonical text equality.
233

290	- rate policy;
291	- authority class.
292	
293	Code changes are warranted for a new wire protocol or a new reusable document/identity primitive—not for a new subject domain.
294	
295	## 7. Politeness scheduler
296	
297	Replace `_HOST_LAST` and the single `SPACING_S` in [acquisition.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/acquisition.py) with a cross-process persistent scheduler:
298	
299	- Per-host token bucket and concurrency limit.
300	- Resolver host and content host budgeted separately; redirects charge the destination host.
301	- Configured limits enforced from `source_routes.yaml`.
302	- Dynamic limits can only reduce the configured rate unless explicitly approved.
303	- Parse both numeric and HTTP-date `Retry-After`.
304	- Store `not_before`; do not sleep and retry three seconds later when the server requests hours.
305	- Cache exact-ID resolver responses; use ETag/Last-Modified where supported.
306	- Batch PMC identifiers.
307	- Circuit-break on repeated 401/403/429/5xx.
308	- Parallelize across independent hosts, not within a host beyond its policy.
309	
310	Outcome semantics:
311	
312	- 429 → `THROTTLED` and deferred.
313	- 401 → `AUTH_FAILED`.
314	- 403 → `ACCESS_DENIED` for that URL.
315	- Timeout/5xx → bounded retry then `BACKEND_FAILED`.
316	- None of these may reduce to “no OA copy exists.”
317	
318	## 8. Yield forecast
319	
320	For the approximately 2,490 candidates with no known OA URL:
321	
322	- Complete, correct-work document manifestations: approximately 450–850, or 18–34%.
323	- Strict journal-VoR-attributable full texts: approximately 180–360, or 7–14%.
324	- Additional accepted-manuscript/preprint leads: approximately 200–400, not counted as strict journal full text.
325	
326	The best working point estimate is roughly 260 additional strict journal-attributable full texts. It is not yet measured strongly enough to use as a commitment.
327	
328	Calibrate with a frozen stratified canary of 200–300 DOI-bearing candidates, split across venue/year/field/access status. Report unique incremental yield after each route, not gross candidates.
329	
330	## 9. Build and release order
331	
332	1. Close the accepted-manuscript P0 and route-lineage bug.
333	2. Add candidate/provenance schema and persistent host scheduler.
334	3. Wire PMC, Europe PMC, and DOAJ.
335	4. Add CORE, OpenAIRE Graph v3, and Zenodo.
336	5. Add generic targeted OAI-PMH.
337	6. Add byte-derived identity, version, completeness, and per-span correspondence.
338	7. Run a cumulative ladder on one frozen corpus, one route addition at a time.
339	8. Only then freeze the enriched corpus and run synthesis comparisons.
340	
341	Required tests:
342	
343	- PMC VoR JATS becomes admissible.
344	- NIH/accepted manuscript remains non-VoR.
345	- The Parry/Yang-Hui He wrong-work case is quarantined.
346	- The Acemoglu 0.37/0.2 mismatch cannot align.
347	- An independently matching VoR span can be rebound.
348	- A short legal judgment is recognized as complete.
349	- A complete registry record does not need article-length prose.
350	- 429 never becomes `SEARCHED_NONE`.
351	- One route cannot inherit another route’s manifestation.
352	- Multiple worker processes remain inside the host budget.
353	- Truncated or HTML-error downloads cannot enter synthesis.

"court", "courts", "judicial", "holding", "liability", "plaintiff", "defendant", "tort",
             "doctrine", "doctrinal", "jurisdiction", "constitutional", "litigation", "precedent",
             "damages", "negligence", "governance", "policy analysis"]

  - role: evidence.trial_registration
    any_of: ["clinical trial", "clinical trials", "randomized controlled", "randomised controlled",
             "rct", "phase iii", "phase ii", "placebo-controlled", "efficacy", "safety", "adverse event",
             "endpoint", "intervention", "treatment arm", "patients", "dose-response"]

  - role: evidence.clinical_guideline
    any_of: ["clinical guideline", "clinical guidelines", "practice guideline", "guidance",
             "recommendation", "recommendations", "standard of care", "cochrane", "systematic review",
             "screening", "management of", "diagnosis and treatment"]

  - role: evidence.case_law
    any_of: ["case law", "case-law", "judicial opinion", "judicial opinions", "court", "courts",
             "holding", "ruling", "rulings", "precedent", "liability", "plaintiff", "defendant",
             "tort", "litigation", "appellate", "supreme court", "damages", "negligence"]

  - role: evidence.statute_regulation

             "compliance", "legal framework", "enacted"]

  - role: evidence.regulatory_guidance

             "supervisory", "central bank", "prudential", "enforcement action", "consultation paper"]

  - role: evidence.standard
    any_of: ["technical standard", "iso ", "iec ", "ieee ", "protocol", "specification",
             "interoperability", "conformance", "reference architecture", "normative"]

  - role: evidence.dataset
